rulings.Case Title: ALBERT H. WONG ET AL VS. THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 2, 2026. Line 1. DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is continued to April 27, 2026 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIN LING ZHANG VS. ELISEO ORTEGON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 2, 2026. Line 5. PLAINTIFF MIN ZHANG Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Agreement is DENIED without prejudice. No competent evidence that the Stipulation is still in effect, that Defendant breached, or that Defendant is still in possession. The declaration of Plaintiff, which is dated January 6, 2025, is not competent to attest to facts and circumstances purportedly occurring several months after this date. Additionally, there is no foundation in the declaration of Plaintiff's counsel for the statements that Defendant failed to make payments or that Defendant is still in possession of the premises. The Court also notes that the proof of electronic service of the moving papers on Defendant's counsel does not comply with CCP 1013b(b).=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENTH AND MARKET, LLC VS. FAYRENE LITTLE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 2, 2026. Line 6. PLAINTIFF TENTH AND MARKET, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Plaintiff's previous Motion for Summary Judgment was denied because there was no competent admissible evidence provided regarding breach or tenant's continued possession of the premises. The present motion addresses the second of these two issues only. Plaintiff offers no new or different facts, circumstances, or law regarding the previous lack of evidence of breach (i.e. nonpayment).=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JONES KONG TOM VS. DEIDRA CAHUA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 2, 2026. Line 8. DEFENDANT DEIDRA CAHUA, PILAR PAYTON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED, subject to opposition. The purported 3-day notice to pay rent or quit attached to Plaintiff's Complaint does not comply with the requirements of CCP 1161(2). The Court notes that, contrary to Defendants' argument, this notice is in writing. Nonetheless, this writing fails to comply with the various specific requirements of the unlawful detainer statutes, most notably CCP 1161(2). Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
